human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest scope, authority-source baseline, scenario suite, evaluation results, deployment notes, marketing notes, and shared-meta-agent delegation contracts against issue #154 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to legacy API facades, event bridges, contract shaping, migration sequencing, and delivery-guarantee reasoning and rejects adjacent middleware, EDI, scheduling, RPA, and broad greenfield lanes.
Real public authoritative sources are documented with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, and decay policy in the manifest.

The package includes specialist-owned scenarios for modernization guidance, legacy constraint handling, integration failure modes, migration-risk prioritization, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and meta-agent fallback behavior.
The manifest defines bootstrap metadata, delegated request and response contracts, and fallback behavior for the shared meta-agents required by issue #154.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment packaging normalization, and commercialization wording review are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffold consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the specialist-owned baseline.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #154.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation, rollback path, monitoring and logging expectations, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without broadening scope.
